title: Cloudflare Workers Script Errors and CPU Time (`cloudflare:json`)
dataSources: Cloudflare Logpush JSON delivered to Splunk HEC/S3 with `sourcetype=cloudflare:json` (HTTP requests, firewall, Workers, DNS, Access as configured)
app: Cloudflare App for Splunk (Splunkbase 4501) + Logpush JSON (`cloudflare:json`)
spl:
index=cdn sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-24h
| where match(_raw,"(?i)Workers|worker")
| spath ScriptName
| spath Outcome
| spath CPUMilliseconds
| stats count avg(CPUMilliseconds) as avg_cpu_ms by ScriptName, Outcome
| where Outcome!="ok" OR avg_cpu_ms>50
| sort -count
description: Workers extend the edge; uncaught exceptions or CPU spikes translate directly to failed user journeys.
value: Developers get SRE-grade telemetry without building a separate metrics pipeline.
implementation: Configure Logpush jobs per dataset; route all into a unified `cloudflare:json` sourcetype or use transforms to normalize. Use `spath` for PascalCase fields. Create macros per zone name.
visualization: Timechart (error rate), table (script, outcome, p95 CPU).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Web.Web
  by Web.user, Web.url, Web.http_method span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
